title: IEC 62366-1 Usability Engineering File Record
description: One use-scenario/evaluation entry suitable for inclusion in a manufacturer's IEC 62366-1 §4.2 usability engineering file. No mdux-governance Rust type mirrors this yet — this schema is a documentation-level recording shape only, deliberately aligned in style with docs/iec62304/schemas/hazard.schema.json and verification-case.schema.json so a future export could reuse the same conventions. See docs/iec62366/01-scope-and-general-requirements.md §4.2 and docs/governance/citation-convention.md.

ue_record_id.description: Sequential, unique across the usability engineering file, e.g. 'UE-001'.

description.description: The use scenario under evaluation, stated against the manufacturer's use specification (IEC 62366-1:2015 §5.1 Use specification).

hazard_related.description: True if this scenario's use error(s) could lead to a hazardous situation (IEC 62366-1:2015 §5.2 Identify frequently used functions and hazard-related use scenarios). Determines whether this scenario is a candidate for summative evaluation.

frequency_of_use.description: Qualitative frequency-of-use classification supporting the §5.2 identification of frequently used functions.

evaluation_type.description: IEC 62366-1:2015 §5.6 Formative evaluation or §5.7 Summative evaluation. See docs/iec62366/03-formative-and-summative-evaluation.md for what MduX-rust's --verify-ui tooling does and does not provide toward either.

evaluation_method.description: How the evaluation was performed, e.g. 'Simulated-use test with representative users', 'Expert/heuristic review', 'mdux --verify-ui inspection-based check (GoldenBounds/ChromeColor/TextPresence/InkContainment)'. Free text: this schema does not constrain method to MduX-rust-generated evidence.

findings.description: Summary of what the evaluation found, including any use errors, close calls, or confirmations of safe/effective use.

use_errors_identified.description: Use errors (IEC 62366-1:2015 §3 Terms and definitions) observed or identified during this evaluation, if any. An empty array is a valid outcome (no use errors identified).

risk_control_measures.description: Risk control measures adopted as a result of this record, ideally naming real mdux_governance::RequirementId values (see crates/mdux-governance/src/lib.rs and docs/iec62304/02-development-planning-and-requirements.md §5.2.2 Include risk control measures in requirements) rather than free-standing mitigation notes.

evaluation_date.description: Optional: when this evaluation was conducted.

evaluators.description: Optional: who conducted or reviewed this evaluation.
